# TESS Program G08195

Title: Refining Hpic Catalog For Searching For Habitable Worlds With Hwo

PI: Airapetian, Vladimir - NASA/GSFC & Adnet

Type: SMALL

Summary: One of the paramount goals of exoplanetary science and astrobiology in general is to gain the capability to directly image rocky (Earth-like) exoplanets in the habitable zones around solarlike (F, G and K type) stars. This will become possible with the application of direct imaging methodology in the upcoming Habitable Worlds Observatory (HWO), which will be capable for the first time to obtain atmospheric signatures from the thermal and reflected spectra of Earth-like exoplanets. The major purpose of the proposed study is to dramatically refine the sample of the Habitable Worlds Observatory Preliminary Input Catalog (HPIC) stars with derived stellar ages by using light curves from TESS 20-sec cadence data with TESS magnitude between 4 to 8 that will be accessible in Sectors 97-107.


# Sector 097 targets observed (2 min: 41, 20 s: 41)
